# Questions in Livestream 93

Live Stream #93: Praying for the People of Turkey

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=313" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">5:13</a> | How can Abraham and the Israelites be called Amorite without contradicting Genesis 10's table of nations? | He said Genesis 10 is giving an ideological map of peoples, not a rigid genetic chart, and assumes long-term intermarriage among groups. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=848" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:08</a> | Is salvation conditional on recognized need and faith, or can Messiah save whomever he pleases regardless? | He rejected both hyper-Calvinist and hyper-Arminian extremes and framed grace as a real invitation that still calls for human response. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=1215" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:15</a> | Would it be interesting to ask ChatGPT Bible questions and critique the responses? | He thought it could be interesting, especially on Exodus questions, but warned that AI tends to give common-knowledge answers rather than specialist judgments. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=1458" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">24:18</a> | Could early daters of the Exodus substantiate their position, and do later-date proponents run into problems? | He said early daters have struggled to publish strong evidence and have not yet presented a compelling case, though he would change views if the evidence warranted it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=1763" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">29:23</a> | Were Horus and Atum ever conflated? | No. He said Atum was merged with Re as a creator/solar deity, but Horus and Atum were not conflated. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=1817" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:17</a> | Is Pharaoh's incarnation of Horus similar to the incarnation of Jesus? | No. He said Pharaoh remains fully human with divine association resting on him, unlike Christ as fully God and fully man. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=2094" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">34:54</a> | What are the reasons for identifying Pithom and Etham as the same place, and does Hoffmeier have valid reasons for rejecting that connection? | He argued the P could drop from Egyptian place names in Dynasty 19, so Pithom and Etham can be the same place without proving late redaction; he thought Hoffmeier went too far in rejecting the connection. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=2501" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:41</a> | Is there anything about ancient Egyptian religion that is especially interesting but not widely known among laypeople? | He highlighted the layered sacred space of Egyptian temples and how different temple zones reflected different degrees of access and holiness. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=2689" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">44:49</a> | Does he agree with Hoffmeier's theory that the Sea of Reeds was in the Bitter Lakes region? | Generally yes. He said they might differ on the precise lake, but both place it in the Pelusiac estuary region. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=2743" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:43</a> | As an Egyptologist and biblical scholar, what does he think of The Prince of Egypt compared with The Ten Commandments? | He remembered The Prince of Egypt as having many problems, while praising The Ten Commandments for strong Egyptian detail despite some filmic and biblical inaccuracies. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=3389" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">56:29</a> | Could manna be explained by plants secreting sugar crystals, and what does he think of William Propp's Exodus commentary? | He said Propp is a serious secular scholar with both good and bad ideas, but he doubted the sugar-crystal explanation and tentatively favored something more like a dawn-appearing fungus. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=3648" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:00:48</a> | Where did Hannah get the idea of an anointed king before Israel had an established monarchy? | He said anointing kings and priests was a known ancient Near Eastern practice, so Hannah could have known the concept from surrounding cultures. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=3825" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:03:45</a> | Why did the spelling shift from older forms like "Thutmosis" to newer forms? | He said the change reflects dissatisfaction with Greek-based spellings and a push toward renderings closer to Egyptian. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=4077" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:07:57</a> | How good is the research of archaeologists who disagree with him on Exodus dating when they work on later periods like the United Monarchy? | He said their work tends to improve closer to the Christian era; he praised some field archaeology while criticizing interpretive handling where presuppositions are stronger. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=4708" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:18:28</a> | Does Judges 5:20 indicate that Deborah believed in the astrological power of stars to determine fate or outcomes? | No. He interpreted the stars as imagery for angelic or spiritual powers, not astrology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=4908" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:21:48</a> | In 2 Samuel 12:31, does David putting the people of Rabbah under saws, sharp instruments, axes, and brick kilns refer to execution or forced labor? | He argued it refers to forced labor, with conquered people oppressed under tools and brickwork rather than executed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=5288" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:28:08</a> | Who is the best candidate for Darius the Mede in Daniel? | He did not give a firm candidate, saying the identity is hotly contested and he had not studied it closely enough to decide. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=5384" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:44</a> | Do legends about Solomon summoning demons mean ancient Jews did not regard demons as absolutely evil? | No. He said the legends portray Solomon as wise enough to command evil beings, not as treating demons as good. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=5568" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:32:48</a> | What should someone do if they are anxious about believing the wrong doctrines of Christianity? | He pointed to core Christian essentials and salvation by grace through faith, while noting more personal background would be needed for specific church advice. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=5857" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:37:37</a> | Can loanwords be used to date biblical texts, or does that argument fail? | He said loanwords can help date texts as vocabulary artifacts, though their usefulness is limited by the smaller evidence pool. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=6041" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:40:41</a> | Is there any credence to Jebel al-Lawz as the true Mount Sinai, or has it been overhyped? | He said it has been overhyped and gave objections from Exodus geography, the depth of the Nuweiba crossing, weak archaeological claims, petroglyph issues, and desert varnish. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=6512" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:48:32</a> | What are his thoughts on Galatians 3:17, how does Paul reach the 430-year number, and why is it important? | He said Paul draws from Exodus 12:40 and likely follows a text tradition that included Egypt and Canaan, aligning the 430 years with Abraham through Sinai. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=6929" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:55:29</a> | Is monophysitism, ascribed to the Coptic church, an error or a valid conclusion about Christ's nature? | He said it is an error because it confuses Christ's human and divine natures, though he added that many modern Copts do not press the old dispute strongly. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=7300" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:01:40</a> | Is it possible that Judas Iscariot repented, or does Jesus' statement imply otherwise? | He distinguished remorse from saving repentance and said John 17:12 makes Judas' destruction clear. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=7792" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:09:52</a> | How would he parse Isaiah 6:1? | He declined to answer the parsing question because the surrounding controversy was too touchy, though he said he would not accept the disputed view being referenced. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=7945" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:12:25</a> | Which "dullard Pharaoh" had he previously mentioned? | He said it was one of the late Ptolemies, perhaps Ptolemy XI, XII, or XIII, but he could not recall exactly. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=8185" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:16:25</a> | Does mimicry in animals imply a form of deceptiveness in the Creator? | He said animal mimicry is not needed to raise the issue, because Scripture already shows deception can be used in morally complex ways; context and purpose matter. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=9142" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:32:22</a> | Can tortillas function as unleavened bread for Passover when matzah is hard to get? | He said homemade tortillas without leavening can function as unleavened bread if prepared properly, though many commercial tortillas contain leavening. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=9414" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:36:54</a> | Should he react to TikTok videos? | He said it might be fun for some odd videos, but the real question is whether he should do it, not whether he could. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=9485" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:38:05</a> | Are the stories of David in the Bible reliable? | He said yes at a base level, while noting debates now focus more on details and how inflated modern expectations shape readings of the David narratives. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=9738" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:42:18</a> | How did Israelites understand natural phenomena such as lightning? Did God cause everything directly or wield nature as something functionally independent? | He said the Old Testament portrays God as transcendent over nature, able to use it without being a god-of-the-gaps directly embedded in natural phenomena. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=10075" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:47:55</a> | Has he heard that Answers in Genesis is going after fellow young earthers called young-earth evolutionists? | He had not followed it closely, but said it would not surprise him because the creationist/evolutionist divide is central for them. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=10163" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:49:23</a> | If the Gospels were collated from prior written sources such as Q, what happened to those sources and why do we not have them? | He said Q is hypothetical and unnecessary for explaining the Synoptic Gospels; what we actually have evidence for is the Gospels themselves. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=10368" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:52:48</a> | Does he have recommendations for how English speakers who know no Hebrew or Greek should pronounce biblical names and places? | He did not know a good resource beyond beginning Hebrew and Greek grammars, and noted pronunciation is hard even with reading competence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=10490" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:54:50</a> | How should Acts 16:31 be understood: does one person's belief save the household, or is the whole household commanded to believe? | He favored the latter, saying the New Testament does not teach that one person's faith saves another. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=10779" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:59:39</a> | Does Acts 13:1 refer to skin color when it calls Simeon "Niger"? | He said it could refer to darkness in some way, but as an epithet it need not indicate race or African descent and should not be overused ideologically. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=11211" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:06:51</a> | If marooned on a desert island with three ancient Egyptian items, what would he choose? | He chose an axe, a spindle for making thread, and Flint blades, because those would help him build shelter, make tools, and make more supplies. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=11442" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:10:42</a> | Is annihilationism strictly Protestant, or has he encountered Orthodox and Catholics who hold it? | He said he has encountered Orthodox and Catholics who hold annihilationism, so it is not strictly Protestant. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=11585" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:13:05</a> | How is there enough time between Terah fleeing Urkesh and Genesis 14 if the Elamite occupation of Mesopotamia lasted only a couple years per the Mari texts? | He said Urkesh traded hands amid Mari, Elamite, and Babylonian conflicts, leaving enough time before the Elamite-Babylonian coalition behind the Genesis 14 setting. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=11902" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:18:22</a> | What script and writing materials would David and Solomon have used? | He answered that they would have used early alphabetic paleo-Hebrew-type script, with ostraca, parchment, and stone depending on the task. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=12136" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:22:16</a> | Does Genesis 1:27 teach that humans were made masculine and feminine simultaneously? | No. He said the verse shifts from a singular "him" to plural "them," anticipating the fuller account of male and female in Genesis 2. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=12298" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:24:58</a> | Does matzah mean suffering? | He did not find "suffering" as the meaning; he said matzah means flatbread/unleavened bread, with separate senses involving strife and an Arabic cognate for tastelessness. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=12859" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:34:19</a> | When does martyrdom become indirect or direct suicide, and why is accepting death sometimes martyrdom rather than suicide? | He said it depends on principle and intent: seeking death for salvation is wrong, but refusing to deny God when death is imposed is martyrdom. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=13254" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:40:54</a> | Can the Genesis genealogies be read over a span as long as 50,000 years? | He said the toledot/genealogical structures are not strict chronology, and honorific or clan-based ordering can leave wide chronological ranges. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Q7XQMH-rmWo?t=13879" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:51:19</a> | What is his view on the devil's power against God? | He said the devil is powerful compared with humans but nothing compared with God, and is ultimately a defeated enemy. |
